title: Odonata
description: Odonata is a boutique collection of ten luxury townhomes at Third and  Pearl Streets, perched at the mouth of Boulder Canyon where the city meets the foothills. Each of the ten residences offers approximately 3,600 square feet of finished living space with floor-to-ceiling windows framing dramatic Flatiron views, expansive outdoor decks on every level, and a private in-unit elevator. Shared amenities include a gated entry, pool, and sundeck. Finishes throughout — hardwood floors, Spanish tile, Italian cabinetry — set a high benchmark, making Odonata one of Boulder's most refined small-scale residential developments, with urban walkability and immediate mountain access in equal measure.

title: 3370 4th Street
description: This is an “upside down” home: the site slopes steeply away from the street. The main living space is on street level, and is accessed from a foot bridge entering into the entry hall. The great room with ceilings that rise toward the views to the south has generous glazed openings on all sides with access to a large deck. This is four bedroom home measuring 4,600 sf.

title: The Mark
description: A collection of eight town homes with rooftop decks and private yards located in downtown Boulder on Pearl Street.

title: 1860 Bluebell
description: A 5,400 sf home with 6 bedroom, eight bathrooms, office with direct access from the street, elevator and four car garage. Spectacular views of Bear mountain are enjoyed from multiple locations inside or from the large roof deck and primary suite deck.

title: 855 Kalmia
description: A 7,900 sf home on one of the most sought after-streets in Boulder. The home features a great room with fourteen foot ceilings, five en suite bedrooms, sauna, and six car garage

title: Picea
description: Construction has begun on this 52 town home project. The following from the marketing campaign captures the essence of this exciting new project: “A vibrant new community rooted on Spruce Street in the heart of downtown Boulder. Picea’s luxury townhomes are designed to keep your feet on the ground and your gaze to the sky. This is Boulder’s version of city living – naturally, a people-first way to live. Walkable, bikeable, dog-friendly, and primed for outdoor living and adventure. Take a walk on the styled side, where elegance and fine finishes set the character for life above the heart of downtown Boulder. With 52 exquisitely designed, four-level luxury townhomes now for sale, Picea offers contemporary, light-filled interiors and spacious rooftop living spaces, sustainably designed to blend modern sophistication with Boulder’s future-minded spirit.”
